_comment: Pinned real-world refactoring corpus (issue #96). Consumed by scripts/corpus-fetch.sh (materialization) and internal/corpus/corpus_test.go (the //go:build corpus lane). Each target pins a real upstream repository at a fixed commit, applies one verifiable refactoring through the refute CLI, and runs a build/typecheck/test command to prove the edit kept the project valid. Add a target by appending an entry; nothing else needs to change.

targets.name: go-x-example-hello
targets.description: golang.org/x/example hello module: cross-file rename of the exported reverse.String function, used from hello.go and the package tests.

targets.name: rust-itoa
targets.description: dtolnay/itoa: rename the public Buffer type. cargo check builds the lib target only, so the criterion bench dev-dependency is not fetched (offline-safe).

targets.name: ts-ky
targets.description: sindresorhus/ky: rename the exported Ky class. The typecheck verify step needs an npm install and is network-gated (REFUTE_CORPUS_ALLOW_NETWORK_VERIFY=1).

targets.name: python-six
targets.description: benjaminp/six: rename the with_metaclass helper in the single-file module. py_compile is a dependency-free syntax verify.

targets.name: java-json
targets.description: stleary/JSON-java: rename the Property.toJSONObject method via OpenRewrite (a method rename, not a type rename, so no source file needs renaming to stay compilable). Requires the built OpenRewrite adapter JAR (REFUTE_OPENREWRITE_JAR); the maven compile verify is network-gated.
